title: L2 MEV Data Availability Survey
why_now: The portability framework identified data availability as the primary bottleneck for cross-chain research extension. Surveying which EVM-compatible L2s (Optimism, Arbitrum, Base, zkSync) publish block attribution, sequencer, and execution trace data in queryable formats would identify the most viable targets for applying the 5 portable methods.

title: Solana Block Producer Concentration Study
why_now: The portability framework classified Methods 1, 5, and 6 (market map, fingerprinting, dependency risk) as portable to integrated-builder chains. Solana publishes validator and leader schedule data publicly. Applying these methods to Solana would serve as a proof-of-concept for the portability framework's predictions.

title: Portable Method Template Library
why_now: The portability framework identified 5 fully portable methods. Extracting these into chain-agnostic templates (with explicit data schema requirements and parameter placeholders) would make cross-chain application systematic rather than ad-hoc. Each template would specify: required data schema, analytical steps, expected outputs, and chain-specific parameters to calibrate.
